D9 BiologyL4 AnalyzeReference

Flavonoid UV Sunscreen — Invisible Skin in the Epidermis

epidermal flavonoid layers absorb UV-B (280-315 nm) before it reaches the photosynthetic mesophyll; plants from alpine and tropical zones invest heavily in these "colourless" UV pigments

Flavonols (kaempferol, quercetin), chalcones, and aurones absorb 280-360 nm strongly. They sit in the upper epidermal cells, blocking UV-B from damaging photosynthetic machinery in mesophyll. UV-stressed (alpine, tropical) plants accumulate them; shaded plants make less. The pigments are colourless to humans — invisible "sunscreen".

Drag latitude. Flavonoid concentration scales with UV-B intensity; leaf appears the same colour in visible while UV absorption tower grows.
